slides.title: One nutrient your *heart and brain* both want.
slides.sub: Omega-3s from fatty fish are linked to healthier aging. A little fish goes a long way.

slides.title: What omega-3s do
slides.body: These fats help build cell walls and *calm inflammation*. Your body cannot make them, so food matters.

slides.title: Kinder to your heart
slides.body: Regular fatty fish is linked to *a healthier heart* and steadier rhythm as you age.

slides.title: Support for memory
slides.body: Omega-3s are a building block of the brain. Higher intake *may help protect* memory over time.

slides.title: Eat fish twice a week
slides.body: Salmon, sardines and mackerel are rich and simple. Two servings a week covers most people.

slides.title: Small habit, big payoff
slides.body: You do not need much. A couple of fish meals a week *quietly supports* aging well.

slides.title: Feed the years that *feel good*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores your food, sleep and movement and turns the habits that protect your healthspan into one daily number.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
One simple habit your heart and brain both love: fatty fish. 🐟

Omega-3s help calm inflammation and are linked to healthier heart and memory aging. Two servings a week covers most people. Save this. 🔖
